findings.claim: D2 correctly uses an integration-test binary with committed fixture tests/fixtures/canonical_cid.txt (bafyr4iflzldgzjrtknevsib24ewiqgtj65pm2ituow3yxfpq57nfmwduda). Genuinely cross-process.
findings.evidence: Fixture is committed; test byte-compares against a freshly computed CID in each fresh cargo test process.
findings.fix: Optional: run d2_cross_process twice sequentially in CI to defend against regressions that leak process-local state.

findings.claim: D3 is a wasm32 compile-check only, not a runtime determinism assertion. No wasm runtime has actually executed the canonical-CID computation and compared byte-for-byte against the fixture.
findings.evidence: SPIKE-phase-1-stack-RESULTS.md line 39: cargo check --target wasm32-unknown-unknown -p benten-core. BLAKE3 spec guarantees identical output across SIMD/portable paths; not yet empirically proved.
findings.fix: Phase 1 CI: run canonical-CID under wasm32-wasip1 (wasmtime run on print_canonical_cid) and assert output matches committed fixture.

findings.claim: Only aarch64-apple-darwin was used to bootstrap the fixture. x86_64-linux / x86_64-darwin not empirically validated.
findings.evidence: SPIKE-phase-1-stack-RESULTS.md does not document multi-arch reproduction.
findings.fix: Phase 1 CI matrix across {aarch64-apple-darwin, x86_64-apple-darwin, x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu} must reproduce the fixture CID.

findings.claim: BTreeMap<String,Value> iterates in UTF-8 lex order, NOT DAG-CBOR length-first canonical order. Determinism is actually from serde_ipld_dagcbor canonicalization at encode. Doc comment slightly misleading.
findings.evidence: lib.rs:22-27 doc says properties iterate in Unicode code point order (BTreeMap order), but on-wire canonicalization is the load-bearing property. serde_ipld_dagcbor source not inspectable in sandbox but is the documented IPLD reference DAG-CBOR impl.
findings.fix: Tighten doc comment; add a test inserting keys of mixed lengths (a vs aa) into Value::Map and assert emitted CBOR bytes are in length-first order, empirically verifying canonicalization.

findings.claim: Agent spec requires proptest coverage (100k instances, round-trip + hash divergence) and a cross-target-determinism feature. Neither exists in the spike.
findings.evidence: grep proptest crates/benten-core: no matches. No such feature flag in Cargo.toml.
findings.fix: Phase 1: add proptest dev-dep and prop_node_roundtrip_cid_stable test; add cross-target-determinism feature exposing golden vectors for CI.

findings.claim: Value lacks Float variant by design; NaN cannot enter. When Float is added in Phase 1+, hash path must reject NaN/Inf and emit DAG-CBOR shortest-form float (or rely on serde_ipld_dagcbor).
findings.evidence: grep f32|f64|Float|NaN on benten-core: only the #[serde(untagged)] hit.
findings.fix: Add tests for NaN rejection, shortest-form encoding, and a proptest over arbitrary f64 bit patterns when Float variant is introduced.

findings.claim: features=[pure] controls only the C fallback per spike notes. Native still uses SIMD intrinsics. Fine because BLAKE3 spec guarantees bit-identical output, but native and WASM take different code paths and must be empirically compared.
findings.evidence: Cargo.lock:151-162 blake3 1.8.4 with cpufeatures optional; cargo tree -p benten-core does not pull cpufeatures into benten-core subtree. SPIKE-phase-1-stack-RESULTS.md 158-164.
findings.fix: Empirical cross-target assertion (covered by the wasm32 runtime CI job).

findings.claim: Hash path uses alloc BTreeMap/String/Vec. No HashMap, no Vec layout reliance, no float math, no transmute. Cross-Rust-version determinism defensible.
findings.evidence: grep HashMap|HashSet|f32|f64 in crates/benten-core: empty. grep unsafe|transmute|to_ne_bytes|swap_bytes: only forbid(unsafe_code).
findings.fix: CI builds at MSRV 1.85 and latest stable; both must reproduce the fixture.

Value.risks: no Float variant; no HashMap; no usize/isize; no SystemTime; forbid(unsafe_code)

cross_target_test.wasm32_unknown_unknown: not yet empirically verified; D3 is compile-check only (Phase 1 task)
cross_target_test.identical: expected yes by BLAKE3/DAG-CBOR spec, not empirically proven across all three targets
